thread.name:
Work on this ticket

Overview

Generalize the existing task-specific comment infrastructure in lib/core/communications/channels/comments/ to support any entity type. This is the foundational work that enables all subsequent migrations and new comment capabilities.

What Changes

1. Schema: Extend ConversationReferenceType enum

File: apps/dashboard/database/prisma/schema/communication.prisma

Add ELIGIBILITY_CHECK, CONTACT, PAYER to the ConversationReferenceType enum

Add manualVerificationNotes String? inline field to EligibilityCheck model

Run pnpm --filter database migrate dev && pnpm --filter database generate

2. Generalize comment helpers

Directory: apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/

Refactor task-specific helpers to accept referenceType + referenceId:

Current (Task-Specific)

Generalized

buildTaskConversationReference(taskId)

buildConversationReference(referenceType, referenceId)

extractTaskReference(conversation)

extractEntityReference(conversation) returning { referenceType, referenceId }

fetchCommentResources(orgId, taskId, ...)

fetchCommentResources(orgId, referenceType, referenceId, ...)

validateCommentAuthorization(orgId, authorId, ...)

validateCommentAuthorization(orgId, referenceType, referenceId, authorId, ...)

createCommentCommunication({ taskId, ... })

createCommentCommunication({ referenceType, referenceId, ... })

listCommentCommunications({ taskId, ... })

listCommentCommunications({ referenceType, referenceId, ... })

3. Entity validation registry

File: apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/entity-validators.ts

Create a Record<ConversationReferenceType, EntityValidator> mapping

Each validator checks the referenced entity exists and user has access

Start with TASK, PATIENT, ELIGIBILITY_CHECK; add CONTACT and PAYER stubs for Phases 3-4

4. Update inbox filter

File: list-conversations.ts in communications bounded context

Replace { referenceType: { not: ConversationReferenceType.TASK } } with { referenceType: { not: { in: ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES } } }

Define ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES constant

Acceptance Criteria

ConversationReferenceType enum includes ELIGIBILITY_CHECK, CONTACT, PAYER

EligibilityCheck model has manualVerificationNotes field

Comment helpers accept any ConversationReferenceType (not just TASK)

Entity validator registry is in place with validators for TASK, PATIENT, ELIGIBILITY_CHECK

Inbox filter excludes all entity-comment reference types

Existing task comments continue to work (no regression)

All checks pass: pnpm lint:fix && pnpm format:fix && pnpm typecheck:native && pnpm lint:custom

References

ADR: Unified Notes System (Phase 1 section)

Current task comment implementation: lib/core/communications/channels/comments/

Communication schema: apps/dashboard/database/prisma/schema/communication.prisma

userMessages.content:
SDLC phase advanced to implementing. Execute the approved plan now.

Implement the planned changes directly in code.

After implementing, signal phaseComplete: true again to trigger mandatory review, UI smoke, and PR steps.

SDLC review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Deep review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Entity validators do not enforce per-user access despite accepting authorId (authorization)
In apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/entity-validators.ts, validators for TASK/PATIENT/ELIGIBILITY_CHECK only check existence within organization and never use `authorId`. This violates the stated requirement that validation must confirm the user has access to the referenced entity. As implemented, any org member can create comments on any entity in-org if they know the ID.
2. [HIGH] Generalized comment mutations discard entity identity by returning empty taskId (api-contract)
The mutation contract remains task-specific (`CommentMutationResult.taskId: string`), and create/update/delete now return `""` for non-TASK references (apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/create-comment.ts, update-comment.ts, delete-comment.ts, types.ts). This loses the referenced entity context and makes downstream cache invalidation/event handling ambiguous or incorrect for non-task comments.

Carmack review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Entity validator registry does not enforce per-entity access control (authorization)
In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/entity-validators.ts`, validators only check entity existence by `organizationId` and never use `authorId` (despite the contract requiring access checks). This violates the stated requirement that each validator confirms both existence and user access, and creates an authorization gap for non-task entity comments.
2. [HIGH] Task delete path now accepts all entity-comment reference types (boundary-regression)
`fetchCommentForDeletion` was widened from `referenceType: TASK` to `referenceType in ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` (`apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/helpers/fetch-for-deletion.ts`). `delete-task-comment` still represents a task-scoped API, so this change lets task-scoped deletion logic operate on non-task comments by ID, breaking bounded-context intent and potentially bypassing future entity-specific policy gates.
3. [MEDIUM] Non-task comment mutations return empty-string `taskId` sentinel (api-contract)
`createCommentCommunication`, `updateCommentCommunication`, and `deleteCommentCommunication` now return `taskId: ""` for non-task references (`create-comment.ts`, `update-comment.ts`, `delete-comment.ts`). Empty-string sentinel values are non-type-safe and can cause incorrect cache invalidation/routing behavior in downstream consumers that treat `taskId` as a real identifier.

tasks.title: Extend communication schema for new reference types
acceptanceCriteria: ConversationReferenceType enum includes TASK, PATIENT, ELIGIBILITY_CHECK, CONTACT, and PAYER as needed for entity comments
Prisma migration and client generation complete without schema errors

tasks.title: Introduce entity validator registry for comment authorization and existence checks
acceptanceCriteria: Registry is keyed by `ConversationReferenceType` (no magic strings)
TASK, PATIENT, and ELIGIBILITY_CHECK validators verify entity existence and caller access
CONTACT and PAYER entries exist as explicit stubs with safe failure behavior or TODO contract for future phases
Comment authorization path uses registry dispatch based on `referenceType`

tasks.title: Validate backward compatibility and run quality gates
acceptanceCriteria: Existing TASK comment create/list/read flows still pass (backward compatibility)
Tests cover at least one non-TASK entity reference path (ELIGIBILITY_CHECK preferred)
`pnpm lint:fix && pnpm format:fix && pnpm typecheck:native && pnpm lint:custom` pass

tasks.title: Refactor comments channel helpers to entity-agnostic signatures
acceptanceCriteria: No `as unknown as` introduced; type-bridge and enum rules remain compliant

tasks.title: Update inbox conversation filtering for generalized entity comments
acceptanceCriteria: `ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` constant is defined and used in inbox query logic
Inbox excludes all entity-comment threads represented in the constant

blockingFindings.title: Entity validators do not enforce per-user access despite accepting authorId
blockingFindings.detail: In apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/entity-validators.ts, validators for TASK/PATIENT/ELIGIBILITY_CHECK only check existence within organization and never use `authorId`. This violates the stated requirement that validation must confirm the user has access to the referenced entity. As implemented, any org member can create comments on any entity in-org if they know the ID.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Implement access-aware checks in each validator (or delegated policy functions) using `authorId` and entity-specific authorization rules. If access cannot be verified for a reference type, fail closed with an authorization error.

blockingFindings.title: Generalized comment mutations discard entity identity by returning empty taskId
blockingFindings.detail: The mutation contract remains task-specific (`CommentMutationResult.taskId: string`), and create/update/delete now return `""` for non-TASK references (apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/create-comment.ts, update-comment.ts, delete-comment.ts, types.ts). This loses the referenced entity context and makes downstream cache invalidation/event handling ambiguous or incorrect for non-task comments.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Replace task-only result fields with a generalized reference payload (e.g. `{ referenceType, referenceId }`) and update callers accordingly. Keep task-specific adapters only at task-boundary procedures if needed.

blockingFindings.title: Entity validator registry does not enforce per-entity access control
blockingFindings.detail: In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/entity-validators.ts`, validators only check entity existence by `organizationId` and never use `authorId` (despite the contract requiring access checks). This violates the stated requirement that each validator confirms both existence and user access, and creates an authorization gap for non-task entity comments.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Implement entity-specific access checks inside each validator (or delegated policy functions), and use `authorId` to enforce read/comment permissions for TASK, PATIENT, and ELIGIBILITY_CHECK before allowing comment creation/list/update/delete.

blockingFindings.title: Task delete path now accepts all entity-comment reference types
blockingFindings.detail: `fetchCommentForDeletion` was widened from `referenceType: TASK` to `referenceType in ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` (`apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/helpers/fetch-for-deletion.ts`). `delete-task-comment` still represents a task-scoped API, so this change lets task-scoped deletion logic operate on non-task comments by ID, breaking bounded-context intent and potentially bypassing future entity-specific policy gates.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Keep task-scoped deletion constrained to `ConversationReferenceType.TASK` in the task procedure path, or require explicit `{ referenceType, referenceId }` in delete input and verify conversation binding matches the requested entity context.

blockingFindings.title: Non-task comment mutations return empty-string `taskId` sentinel
blockingFindings.detail: `createCommentCommunication`, `updateCommentCommunication`, and `deleteCommentCommunication` now return `taskId: ""` for non-task references (`create-comment.ts`, `update-comment.ts`, `delete-comment.ts`). Empty-string sentinel values are non-type-safe and can cause incorrect cache invalidation/routing behavior in downstream consumers that treat `taskId` as a real identifier.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Replace `taskId` sentinel with a discriminated return contract containing `{ referenceType, referenceId }`, or make `taskId` nullable/optional and ensure callers branch explicitly on `referenceType`.

deep.title: Entity validators do not enforce per-user access despite accepting authorId
Generalized comment mutations discard entity identity by returning empty taskId

carmack.title: Entity validator registry does not enforce per-entity access control
Task delete path now accepts all entity-comment reference types
Non-task comment mutations return empty-string `taskId` sentinel

payloadSummary.planText: Generalize comment infrastructure from TASK-only references to entity-agnostic references by expanding schema enums, introducing a validator registry keyed by ConversationReferenceType, refactoring helper APIs to take {referenceType, referenceId}, and updating inbox filtering to exclude all entity-comment reference types while preserving TASK behavior.
